items.title: Brent oil futures are holding largely steady around $88 a barrel after a report that the IEA pr…
items.content:
Brent oil futures are holding largely steady around $88 a barrel after a report that the IEA proposed a record oil reserve release. This follows on from a particularly volatile session for oil on Tuesday amid confusion over flows through the Strait of Hormuz. European equity futures have ebbed lower in recent trade with the Euro Stoxx 50 contract falling 0.5%. Risk mood has been dented by an FT report that JPMorgan is marking down private credit portfolios. This has taken some of the shine off US stock futures, which had been lifted by Oracle’s post-earnings surge. Asia was upbeat with the MSCI APAC index climbing 1.2%.

The Bloomberg Dollar index continues to hand back gains, sliding for a fourth consecutive session. The Aussie dollar is top of the G-10 leaderboard as traders anticipate another interest-rate hike from the RBA next week. Bunds are lower by around 20 ticks. US yields are down 1-2bps across the curve after Tuesday’s increases. Precious metals trade is directionless with spot gold up 0.2% and silver down 0.4%. Bitcoin is lower by 0.5%.
布蘭特原油期貨在國際能源署（IEA）提議釋出創紀錄石油儲備的報導出爐後，大致持穩於每桶 88 美元附近。此前週二油市經歷特別動盪的交易日，因霍爾木茲海峽的石油運輸情況引發混亂。歐洲股市期貨在近期交易中回落，Euro Stoxx 50 合約下跌 0.5%。
市場風險情緒受到《金融時報》報導衝擊，該報導指出 JPMorgan 正在下調私人信貸投資組合的估值。這使得美國股市期貨的漲勢有所減弱，此前該漲勢是由 Oracle 公布財報後的股價飆升所帶動。亞洲股市表現樂觀，MSCI APAC 指數上漲 1.2%。Bloomberg Dollar 指數持續回吐漲幅，連續第四個交易日下滑。
澳幣在 G-10 貨幣中表現最佳，因交易員預期澳洲央行（RBA）下週將再次升息。德國公債下跌約 20 個跳動點。美國公債殖利率在週二上漲後，全線下滑 1-2 基點。貴金屬交易缺乏明確方向，現貨黃金上漲 0.2%，白銀下跌 0.4%。比特幣下跌 0.5%。 @parisprogram

items.title: A deeper dive into the mechanics of Wednesday’s Japanese bond auction suggests that the sale ma…
items.content:
A deeper dive into the mechanics of Wednesday’s Japanese bond auction suggests that the sale may not have been as successful as it seems on first sight — highlighting how traders continue to be deeply skeptical of the outlook for JGBs against the current macroeconomic backdrop.

The bid-to-cover ratio was the best since last October as Mark Cranfield points out. However, the 1.6% coupon note actually offers a USD-equivalent yield of 3.88% after a cross-currency swap, meaning it is trading at a discount to the comparable unhedged Treasury maturity.

Japan’s inflation — already a problem for the central bank — will worsen in the months to come from spiraling energy prices for a nation that is reliant almost entirely on imports for its oil needs, and that will only add to recent losses in JGBs.
深入分析週三日本公債標售的運作機制後發現，這場拍賣或許不如表面看來成功——凸顯出在市場當前總體經濟背景下，交易員對日本公債（JGBs）前景仍抱持高度懷疑。正如 Mark Cranfield 所指出的，投標倍數創下去年 10 月以來最佳表現。
然而，這檔票面利率 1.6% 的債券在進行跨貨幣交換後，實際上可提供相當於美元計價的 3.88% 殖利率，這意味著其交易價格相較於可比較的未避險美國公債到期殖利率存在折價。日本的通膨——對該國央行而言已是棘手問題——在未來數月將因能源價格飆升而惡化，而這個幾乎完全依賴進口石油的國家，其日本公債（JGBs）近期的跌勢只會因此雪上加霜。 @parisprogram

items.title: The willingness to buy the recent dips in equities is increasing as crude prices retreat below…
items.content:
The willingness to buy the recent dips in equities is increasing as crude prices retreat below $90 a barrel, with Korea leading major Asian markets higher. This rebound likely reflects short‑term relief rather than a shift in underlying conditions, with the regional outlook increasingly shaped by structural risks tied to energy, policy constraints and positioning.

Tech is leading the rally on the back of better-than-expected earnings from Oracle. However, North Asia’s major economies are net oil importers with a heavy reliance on Middle East energy flows, leaving growth exposed to sustained energy shocks. Higher oil and gas prices also threaten to lift inflation expectations, forcing central banks into an uncomfortable choice between tolerating further currency weakness or tightening policy into slowing growth. As Ven Ram argued yesterday, the starting point matters as well: the BOJ was already seen as behind the curve on inflation before the energy shock.

Positioning presents a further vulnerability. The Kospi is up close to 36% year‑to‑date and has rallied roughly 12% over the past five sessions alone. Even without another leg higher in crude, Brent remains roughly 45% above where it began the year. Equity prices appear to have only partially absorbed the implications of higher energy costs and increased geopolitical risks, leaving scope for renewed pressure should sentiment reverse.
隨著原油價格回落至每桶 90 美元以下，市場逢低買入股票的意願正在增強，韓國引領主要亞洲市場走高。這波反彈可能反映的是短期情緒緩解，而非基本面條件的轉變，區域前景日益受到與能源、政策限制及市場持倉相關的結構性風險所影響。科技股在 Oracle 優於預期的盈餘帶動下領漲。
然而，北亞主要經濟體均為石油淨進口國，高度依賴中東能源供應，使其經濟成長面臨持續能源衝擊的風險。油價與天然氣價格上漲亦可能推高通膨預期，迫使各國央行在容忍本幣進一步貶值，或於經濟放緩之際緊縮貨幣政策之間，陷入兩難抉擇。
正如 Ven Ram 昨日所言，起點同樣重要：在能源衝擊發生之前，BOJ（日本央行）早已被認為在通膨問題上落後於形勢。持倉部位構成進一步的脆弱性。Kospi 指數今年以來已上漲近 36%，僅過去五個交易日就大漲約 12%。即使原油不再進一步走高，Brent 原油價格仍較年初水準高出約 45%。
股價似乎僅部分反映了能源成本上升與地緣政治風險加劇的影響，一旦市場情緒逆轉，仍有進一步承壓的空間。 @parisprogram

items.title: The US dollar’s rebound amid the conflict in the Middle East risks losing momentum unless there…
items.content:
The US dollar’s rebound amid the conflict in the Middle East risks losing momentum unless there is a sudden resurgence in crude prices -- in part because the allure of its peers will likely increase on bets other central banks will hike interest rates while the Federal Reserve considers lowering them.

That’s a dynamic which has helped lift AUD/USD to the highest level since June 2022. The Aussie’s strength is at least partly fueled by the potential boost for an economy that is a net energy exporter, as well as a major supplier of precious and base metals. It’s also benefiting as the RBA leads the way for major central banks in their pivot toward tightening policy.

The greenback jumped since the Iran war began, thanks to its reserve currency role and the US’s status as a major oil and gas producer. However, While the Fed has also signaled concerns about inflation pressures, policy easing remains on the table there. The US is the only developed economy where traders are fully pricing in a cash rate cut over the coming year.
This time round central banks are expected to be more willing to react aggressively to contain inflation pressures, but the muddier economic and political outlook for the Fed means investors are unlikely to anticipate that either Chair Powell or his successor will play any sort of lead role in 2026. That will add to the weak underlying fundamentals to act as a drag on the dollar.
美元在中東衝突期間的反彈可能失去動能，除非油價突然大幅上漲——部分原因是隨著其他央行押注升息而 Fed（聯準會）考慮降息，其同業貨幣的吸引力可能隨之提升。這樣的動態已推動 AUD/USD 升至 2022 年 6 月以來最高水準。
澳幣走強，部分原因在於澳洲作為能源淨出口國以及貴金屬與基本金屬主要供應國，其經濟可能因此受惠。此外，隨著 RBA（澳洲央行）率先引領主要央行轉向緊縮政策，澳幣亦獲得支撐。美元自伊朗戰爭爆發以來大漲，受益於其儲備貨幣地位，以及美國作為主要石油與天然氣生產國的角色。
然而，儘管 Fed（聯準會）也對通膨壓力表達了擔憂，政策寬鬆仍在其考量範圍內。美國是唯一一個市場交易員已完全反映未來一年將降息的已開發經濟體。
這一次，各國央行預計將更願意積極採取行動以遏制通膨壓力，但 Fed 所面臨的經濟與政治前景更為混沌不明，意味著投資人不太可能預期主席 Powell 或其繼任者將在 2026 年扮演任何領導角色。這將加劇美元疲弱的根本基本面，進而對美元形成拖累。 @parisprogram

items.title: AUD/JPY continues to grind higher seemingly immune to the cross-asset turbulence instigated by…
items.content:
AUD/JPY continues to grind higher seemingly immune to the cross-asset turbulence instigated by the Middle East crisis.

Investors are focusing on the contrasting outlook for interest rates in Australia and Japan. Next week’s RBA meeting is being hyped up as a back-to-back rate hike after hawkish comments this week from Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser.

In the meantime, the Bank of Japan still hasn’t laid the groundwork for its next rate increase with the usual string of local media reports ahead of a policy meeting. Moreover, typically there needs to be a long runway of political voices working in tandem with central bank speakers to prepare Japanese investors that a hike is imminent.

That’s playing out in a widening yield spread between AUD and JPY curves, which is underpinning the Aussie dollar’s edge.
AUD/JPY 持續緩步走高，似乎對中東危機引發的跨資產市場動盪免疫。投資人正聚焦澳洲與日本利率前景的反差。澳洲央行（RBA）下週會議被炒作為可能連續升息，因副總裁 Andrew Hauser 本週發表鷹派言論。
與此同時，日本央行（Bank of Japan）仍未透過政策會議前慣常的本地媒體報導串，為下一次升息鋪路。此外，通常需要有長期的政治人物發聲與央行官員相互配合，才能讓日本投資者做好心理準備，得知升息即將來臨。
這正體現在澳幣與日圓殖利率曲線之間不斷擴大的利差上，而這正是支撐澳幣走強的關鍵因素。 @parisprogram

items.title: 🟢 Asia’s emerging economies are struggling to secure prompt shipments for liquefied natural gas…
items.content:
🟢 Asia’s emerging economies are struggling to secure prompt shipments for liquefied natural gas, a sign that global supplies are tightening as the conflict in the Middle East disrupts exports.

The Iran war has shut the world’s largest LNG export facility in Qatar and halted traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. To make up for lost supply, Indian importers have tried -- and failed -- to buy March shipments via a string of tenders. Buyers are now looking to procure for April delivery, which has resulted in shipments destined for Europe diverting to Asia.

Given that Asia competes directly with Europe for LNG cargoes, the more shipments it pulls from the Atlantic, the tighter the global market becomes, pushing costs higher. Spot LNG prices in Asia nearly doubled in the past week, as the region tries to attract more deliveries. This dynamic could intensify the longer the conflict continues.
亞洲新興經濟體正難以確保液化天然氣的即時出貨，這顯示隨著中東衝突擾亂出口，全球供應正在趨緊。伊朗戰爭已關閉卡達全球最大的液化天然氣出口設施，並使荷姆茲海峽的交通中斷。為彌補供應損失，印度進口商已嘗試透過一連串招標購買 3 月出貨，但未能成功。
買家現在正尋求採購4月交運的貨物，導致原本運往歐洲的船貨轉向亞洲。由於亞洲與歐洲在液化天然氣（LNG）船貨上直接競爭，亞洲從大西洋盆地拉走的船貨越多，全球市場就越趨緊俏，進而推升成本。亞洲現貨LNG價格在過去一週幾乎翻倍，因為該地區試圖吸引更多交運量。
這種動態可能會隨著衝突持續時間延長而加劇。 @parisprogram

items.title: Declines in crude prices may be only temporary even if the IEA authorizes its largest-ever rele…
items.content:
Declines in crude prices may be only temporary even if the IEA authorizes its largest-ever release of strategic oil reserves. That’s because the crude stockpiles available will struggle to cover the supply shocks hitting global markets unless the Strait of Hormuz can be reopened fast enough to allow for a rapid restoration of substantial oil flows.

The IEA has proposed a release that would exceed the 182m barrels put into the market in 2022 after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing officials familiar with the matter. That would align with Bloomberg Intelligence estimates that the G-7 could release 360m barrels this time round, or 2m a day for 6 months.

The problem is that would fall well short of replacing the potential loss of 25m barrels a day caused by the Iran conflict, as analysts Henik Fung and Anthony Chen outline.
While crude futures are swinging around rapidly on shifting outlooks for the war itself and efforts to address the impacts on energy costs, the situation in the waterway has changed little. Unless traffic through Hormuz starts to climb rapidly back toward pre-war levels, energy prices will remain elevated as the war, and the effective closure of the strait, chokes off supplies and leads to more and more output cuts.
即使 IEA（國際能源署）批准史上最大規模的戰略石油儲備釋出，原油價格下跌可能也只是暫時現象。這是因為現有的原油庫存將難以彌補衝擊全球市場的供應中斷，除非荷姆茲海峽能夠迅速重新開放，讓大量石油流量得以快速恢復。
據《華爾街日報》引述知情人士報導，IEA 提議釋出的規模將超過 2022 年俄羅斯入侵烏克蘭後向市場投放的 1.82 億桶。這與 Bloomberg Intelligence 的估計相符，即 G-7 這次可能釋出 3.6 億桶，相當於每日 200 萬桶、持續 6 個月。
問題在於，這將遠遠不足以彌補伊朗衝突可能造成的每日 2,500 萬桶損失，正如分析師 Henik Fung 與 Anthony Chen 所指出的。儘管原油期貨因戰爭前景本身以及應對能源成本影響的努力而不斷快速波動，但該水道的局勢幾乎沒有改變。
除非經由 Hormuz 海峽的運輸量開始迅速回升至戰前水準，否則能源價格將持續居高不下，因為戰爭以及該海峽的實質封鎖將扼殺供應，並導致越來越多的產量削減。 @parisprogram

JGB futures are softer amid pre-auction hedging for today’s 5-year sale with traders spooked by a tepid three-year Treasury auction overnight.

However, the Bank of Japan hasn’t been giving any clear signal about the timing of its next rate hike. This should mean the JGB auction passes smoothly. Indeed, the belly of the curve is the pivot area for curve steepening strategies which will provide additional support for the 5-year sector.

That sets up JGB contracts for a short-covering bump higher once the auction details are out. Especially if declines in oil prices on a report about the IEA’s proposal sustain throughout the rest of Wednesday.

Macro traders are having a strong knee-jerk reaction across assets, led by a falling dollar and climbing equities in response to lower WTI futures. There is also a modest bid for Treasury contracts following the WSJ report.

However, this is a low-conviction environment with investors nursing the P&L effects of whipsaw moves since the Iran war started. It is unlikely that the positive mood is durable as traders may use the pop to further de-risk portfolios.

items.title: Global bonds face further declines because the inflationary impact of the US-Iran conflict has…
items.content:
Global bonds face further declines because the inflationary impact of the US-Iran conflict has traders anticipating that major central banks will respond by tightening monetary conditions. That narrative may be gathering extra momentum as investors look for policymakers to avoid the perceived errors of 2022 when “team transitory” was slow to hike rates after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine turbocharged already accelerating inflation.

The RBA’s deputy governor sounded hawkish enough late Tuesday for OIS traders and economists to decide next week’s meeting is more likely than not to result in a back-to-back increase in the cash rate. Two rate hikes within 6 months are now priced for Australia, while the BOJ, ECB, Norges Bank, Denmark’s central bank and the RBNZ are seen as candidates to also tighten.
That underscores the upward skew for global yields, and may also have played a role in the softness of Tuesday’s 3-year Treasuries offering. Longer-end bonds may ultimately reap the benefits if central bank hawkishness ensures inflation expectations and even shades into significant economic slowdowns. But in the short term this narrative will add to the reasons for investors to be reluctant about adding sovereign debt.
ECB President Lagarde’s recent comments about learning the lessons of 2022-3 will act to underscore that theme. Central banks were way behind market expectations in raising interest rates back then, following a long period when inflation undershot forecasts. While oil is swinging around wildly as the US and other developed nations seek ways to revive supplies, central banks are focusing on the likelihood energy costs will remain elevated for some time.

After prices soared earlier this decade -- well above projections -- the RBA’s Hauser signaled Australia’s central bank is willing to respond rapidly this time if needed.

“It’s worth us continuously reminding ourselves just how toxic inflation is. We’ve only just had an experience of that and we don’t want to go through that period again.”
-- RBA Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser.

The RBA led the way this year for major central banks facing a revival in inflation pressures by hiking in February. His comments just a week before Australia’s next rates decision will reverberate across developed bond markets to add to the pressure for higher yields.
全球債券面臨進一步下跌，因為美國與伊朗衝突所帶來的通膨影響，使交易員預期主要央行將以緊縮貨幣政策作為回應。隨著投資人期望政策制定者避免重蹈2022年「暫時性通膨派」的錯誤——當時俄羅斯入侵烏克蘭推升了原本已在加速的通膨，但該派別卻遲遲未能升息——這一論述可能正獲得額外的動能。
澳洲央行（RBA）副總裁週二晚間釋出鷹派訊息，足以讓隔夜指數交換（OIS）交易員和經濟學家認為，下週會議極有可能連續升息。市場目前已完全反映澳洲將在六個月內兩度升息，而日本央行（BOJ）、歐洲央行（ECB）、挪威央行（Norges Bank）、丹麥央行以及紐西蘭央行（RBNZ）也被視為可能跟進緊縮的候選者。
這突顯了全球殖利率向上偏斜的趨勢，也可能導致週二 3 年期美國公債發行表現疲軟的原因之一。若央行鷹派立場能確保通膨預期受控，甚至進一步演變為顯著的經濟放緩，長天期債券最終可能因此受惠。但短期而言，這樣的論述將增添投資人對於增持主權債務的猶豫理由。
ECB 總裁 Lagarde 近期關於汲取 2022-2023 年教訓的言論，將進一步凸顯這一主題。當時央行在升息方面遠遠落後於市場預期，此前通膨長期低於預測水準。
儘管美國及其他已開發國家正尋求恢復供應的方法，導致油價劇烈波動，但各國央行正關注能源成本可能持續維持高檔一段時間的可能性。在本年代稍早價格飆升——遠高於預測水準——之後，RBA（澳洲央行）的 Hauser 暗示，澳洲央行這次如有必要，願意迅速做出回應。「值得我們不斷提醒自己，通膨的毒性究竟有多強。」
「我們才剛經歷過那種情況，不想再重蹈覆轍。」
——RBA 副總裁 Andrew Hauser。RBA 今年在面對通膨壓力回升的主要央行中率先採取行動，於二月升息。他在澳洲下一次利率決策前一周發表的這番言論，將在已開發國家債券市場引發迴響，進一步推高殖利率的壓力。 @parisprogram

items.title: Asian stocks look set for another shaky session after TSMC’s ADRs slipped overnight, with 2026…
items.content:
Asian stocks look set for another shaky session after TSMC’s ADRs slipped overnight, with 2026 sales growth tracking below expectations. That’s a clear negative read-through for regional chipmakers, even as the SOX index managed a modest rebound in the US.

Moreover, Oracle’s jump in afterhours action isn’t doing much to boost US equity futures as WTI crude advances early Wednesday in Asia.

Once again investors will be looking to South Korean stocks to set the tone. Inflows are positive into Korean-focused ETFs amid the intense day-to-day volatility of the Kospi index since the Iran conflict began. (h/t Anthony Stephens.) However, the price action for the equity gauge this week points to ongoing liquidation by macro traders as cross-asset correlations break down.

Net-net: brace for more whipsaw moves. Middle East headlines are driving the tape, and with oil climbing, investors are de facto being forced into the role of energy analysts -- whether they like it or not.
亞洲股市料將再度震盪，因台積電 ADR 隔夜下挫，2026 年營收成長預估低於預期。這對區域晶片製造商顯然是負面訊號，即便美國費城半導體指數（SOX index）已溫和反彈。此外，Oracle 盤後大漲也未能提振美國股指期貨，而西德州中質原油（WTI crude）週三亞洲早盤持續走高。
投資者再次將目光投向韓國股市以定調市場氛圍。自伊朗衝突爆發以來，Kospi 指數日內波動劇烈，但聚焦韓國的 ETF 持續迎來資金淨流入。（感謝 Anthony Stephens 提供資訊。）然而，本週該股票指標的價格走勢顯示，隨著跨資產相關性瓦解，宏觀交易員持續進行平倉操作。總結而言：請為更多劇烈震盪走勢做好準備。
中東地區頭條新聞主導市場走勢，隨著油價攀升，投資者無論是否情願，實際上都被迫扮演起能源分析師的角色。 @parisprogram

items.title: If sustained, the positive reaction to Oracle’s earnings should be good news for big tech stock…
items.content:
If sustained, the positive reaction to Oracle’s earnings should be good news for big tech stocks as it reflects a surprising degree of investor tolerance for a set of merely solid results. The company’s competitors have seen their stocks sink after more impressive quarters, but Oracle is out on its own in post-market trading, up more than 8%.

Whether it can sustain these gains is the big question. After its September earnings print, when it missed estimates on cloud revenue but said remaining performance obligations had swelled to $455b from $138b, Oracle’s shares surged 37% in a day. They have since given all of those gains back -- and much more -- amid concerns about the pace of AI spending.

Revenue in Oracle’s key cloud infrastructure business beat estimates by 3.4% in the three months through November. Remaining performance obligations rose to $553b from $523b three months earlier. The company’s 2027 fiscal revenue projection of $90b came in modestly above analysts’ forecasts.

Executives on the call also sought to quash fears that AI will kill the Software as a Service industry, or at least, Oracle’s SaaS business. They also argued that data centers are profitable, and becoming more so as the company gets better at reducing construction costs.

As the Iran war whiplashes oil prices and equities sentiment, and with AI optimism more fragile than it was in 2025, investors will be pondering whether Oracle’s guidance offers sufficient foundations for tech stocks more broadly to break higher.
若 Oracle 財報所引發的正面反應得以延續，對大型科技股而言應屬佳音，因這反映出投資人對於僅屬穩健的業績表現，竟展現出令人意外的容忍度。該公司同業即便繳出更亮眼的季度成績，股價仍應聲下挫，但 Oracle 在盤後交易中獨樹一幟，漲幅超過 8%。能否維持這些漲幅，則是最大問號。
Oracle 在 9 月公布財報後，當時雖然雲端營收未達預期，但表示剩餘履約義務已從 1,380 億美元膨脹至 4,550 億美元，該公司股價單日飆漲 37%。然而，隨著市場對 AI 支出步調的疑慮升溫，這些漲幅已全數回吐——甚至跌得更深。在截至 11 月的三個月內，Oracle 核心雲端基礎設施業務的營收超出預期 3.4%。
剩餘履約義務從三個月前的 $523b 上升至 $553b。該公司 2027 財年營收預測為 $90b，略高於分析師預期。參與電話會議的高層也試圖平息市場對人工智慧將摧毀軟體即服務產業、或至少 Oracle SaaS 業務的疑慮。他們並主張資料中心具有獲利能力，且隨著公司降低建造成本的能力提升，獲利表現將持續改善。
隨著伊朗戰爭引發油價與股市情緒劇烈波動，且人工智慧樂觀情緒較 2025 年更為脆弱，投資人將思考 Oracle 的財測是否足以為科技股更廣泛地向上突破提供足夠基礎。 @parisprogram

items.content:
WTI crude contracts are extending late session gains from Tuesday, with US equity futures modestly higher.

Although Oracle shares climbed in late trading after solid earnings, that isn’t having much impact on the broader market. Indeed, its bounce looks like short covering as the shares had been on a downward path for several months.

Treasury futures are steady with USD/JPY above 158 ahead of a 5-year JGB auction. WTI is at about $87.50 after hitting as low as $76.73 overnight.
WTI 原油期貨延續週二尾盤漲勢，美國股指期貨小幅走高。

儘管 Oracle 在公布穩健盈餘後於盤後交易上漲，但對整體市場影響有限。事實上，其反彈看似空頭回補，因該股數月來持續走跌。

美國公債期貨持穩，美元兌日圓（USD/JPY）位於 158 上方，適逢 5 年期日本公債（JGB）標售前。WTI 原油在隔夜一度跌至 $76.73 後，目前約報 $87.50。 @parisprogram

items.content: 這突顯了全球殖利率向上偏斜的趨勢，也可能導致週二 3 年期美國公債發行表現疲軟的原因之一。若央行鷹派立場能確保通膨預期受控，甚至進一步演變為顯著的經濟放緩，長天期債券最終可能因此受惠。但短期而言，這樣的論述將增添投資人對於增持主權債務的猶豫理由。

items.content: 澳洲央行（RBA）副總裁週二晚間釋出鷹派訊息，足以讓隔夜指數交換（OIS）交易員和經濟學家認為，下週會議極有可能連續升息。市場目前已完全反映澳洲將在六個月內兩度升息，而日本央行（BOJ）、歐洲央行（ECB）、挪威央行（Norges Bank）、丹麥央行以及紐西蘭央行（RBNZ）也被視為可能跟進緊縮的候選者。

items.content: 全球債券面臨進一步下跌，因為美國與伊朗衝突所帶來的通膨影響，使交易員預期主要央行將以緊縮貨幣政策作為回應。隨著投資人期望政策制定者避免重蹈2022年「暫時性通膨派」的錯誤——當時俄羅斯入侵烏克蘭推升了原本已在加速的通膨，但該派別卻遲遲未能升息——這一論述可能正獲得額外的動能。

items.content: 亞洲股市料將再度震盪，因台積電 ADR 隔夜下挫，2026 年營收成長預估低於預期。這對區域晶片製造商顯然是負面訊號，即便美國費城半導體指數（SOX index）已溫和反彈。此外，Oracle 盤後大漲也未能提振美國股指期貨，而西德州中質原油（WTI crude）週三亞洲早盤持續走高。

items.content: 剩餘履約義務從三個月前的 $523b 上升至 $553b。該公司 2027 財年營收預測為 $90b，略高於分析師預期。參與電話會議的高層也試圖平息市場對人工智慧將摧毀軟體即服務產業、或至少 Oracle SaaS 業務的疑慮。他們並主張資料中心具有獲利能力，且隨著公司降低建造成本的能力提升，獲利表現將持續改善。

items.content: Oracle 在 9 月公布財報後，當時雖然雲端營收未達預期，但表示剩餘履約義務已從 1,380 億美元膨脹至 4,550 億美元，該公司股價單日飆漲 37%。然而，隨著市場對 AI 支出步調的疑慮升溫，這些漲幅已全數回吐——甚至跌得更深。在截至 11 月的三個月內，Oracle 核心雲端基礎設施業務的營收超出預期 3.4%。

items.content: 若 Oracle 財報所引發的正面反應得以延續，對大型科技股而言應屬佳音，因這反映出投資人對於僅屬穩健的業績表現，竟展現出令人意外的容忍度。該公司同業即便繳出更亮眼的季度成績，股價仍應聲下挫，但 Oracle 在盤後交易中獨樹一幟，漲幅超過 8%。能否維持這些漲幅，則是最大問號。

items.content: 這種緊張局勢在週二的一系列頭條新聞中表露無遺。能源部長 Chris Wright 在 X 平台發文稱，美國護送一艘油輪穿越荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz）。原油價格應聲重挫。數分鐘後，該貼文消失，隨後遭白宮否認，油價隨即反彈。盤後時段，相互矛盾的報導持續出現。在紛擾之下，實體供應的緊縮情況正日益惡化。

items.content: 💥 即便在亞洲交易時段從週一高點大幅回落後，原油選擇權仍對於大幅日內波動定價，且 Brent 與 WTI 的買權偏斜度均處於極高水準。伊朗衝突造就了一個「僅差一則頭條」的市場，回檔幾乎無法消除供應衝擊的尾部風險。原油週二在兩種相互競爭的敘事之間震盪。

items.content: 🟢 投資者在伊朗戰爭引發的石油衝擊有望受控的預期重燃之際，正謹慎重返信貸市場。然而，他們尚未準備好擁抱風險最高的標的。槓桿貸款已從 2023 年低點反彈，這是市場趨於穩定的初步跡象，而初級市場在早先因 AI 擔憂引發的價格暴跌導致發行放緩後，如今也展現出復甦活力，且這一切甚至在戰爭爆發前就已發生。

items.content: 若能源價格持續居高不下，ECB（歐洲央行）與 BOE（英國央行）升息的潛在可能性將成為市場主軸，波動性也將較美國獲得更好的支撐。若伊朗風險溢價消退，首波反應更可能是波動性下滑，而非殖利率出現明確的反彈行情，因市場將先對尾部風險情境進行重新定價，之後才會恢復現貨方向的押注。

items.content: 風險限額收緊，原本樂於賣出波動率的交易部門規模縮小，甚至完全退場。這使得隱含波動率即使在實際波動率降溫時仍維持高檔，也讓跨市場套利與收斂策略變得更加脆弱。美國並非免疫，但起點不同。能源衝擊對歐洲與英國的影響更為直接，而美國的勞動市場背景仍促使 Fed（聯準會）傾向降息而非升息。

items.content: 這對波動率而言至關重要，因為舊有的均衡狀態仰賴於政策路徑結果的窄區間分布。一旦升息尾部風險浮現，做空 Gamma 交易便不再是被動收入，而開始具備危及交易員職涯的潛力。其連鎖效應往往持續存在。在交易員被迫平倉後，波動率供給可能不會迅速反彈。

items.content: 在英國，這一轉變導致政策路徑遭到嚴厲重新定價，劇烈拋售對陡峭化交易及前端相對價值交易衝擊最為嚴重。歐洲面臨同樣問題，且對天然氣衝擊的曝險更為直接。ECB（歐洲央行）官員一直試圖展現沉著態度，但市場仍持續朝今年內升息的方向定價。
